title: wrightia laniti
intro: Wrightia laniti, also known as Wrightia tomentosa, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly found in Southeast Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments. The plant's leaves, roots, and stems are rich in alkaloids and flavonoids, which are believed to have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, offering health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving coughs, and improving respiratory health. The plant's herbal preparations include extracts, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to treat various conditions, including respiratory infections, skin conditions, and digestive issues.
